violations.text: The single door lowboy under the menu was observed in poor repair (door not sealing/tightly fitted). Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2025

The non-food contact surfaces were observed with: 1) The ice machine was observed with pink residue on the front side of the ice guard. 2) The bottom of the back prep table was observed with an accumulation of food debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned as often as necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2025

At the time of re-inspection, the 2 Door Low Boy Cooler Unit nearest the drive-thru window was observed to be taken out of service with a printed sign on doors instructing not to use. Per the PIC, unit was serviced since previous inspection. Unit was monitored for proper cold holding ambient temperature since service and was observed to not maintain proper temperature. Per the PIC, unit was taken out of service and is to remain out of service until fixed/replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023

At the time of inspection, the hand sink in the back room area was observed to have food debris inside it. A HANDWASHING SINK may not be used for purposes other than handwashing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2023

Several Milk/Cream items in the 2 Door Low Boy Cooler at the drive thru window were observed held at 48 to 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per the PIC, Milk items were in refrigeration since previous day. Milk items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2023

At the time of inspection, the following equipment located in the establishment was observed in poor repair/adjustment... - The 2 Door Low Boy Cooler at the Drive Thru Window was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Use of the 2 Door Low Boy Cooler for Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods is to be discontinued until serviced/repaired. - The Low Boy Cooler nearest the donut display was observed to have a door that is not closing/sealing properly. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3 Bay Sink is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2023
